sections.heading: How I Think About Collaboration in the Agent Era
sections.description: Current questions spanning multi-human steering, agent-driven engineering, and faster software evolution.

sections.heading: Selected Papers from Earlier Work
sections.description: Selected papers on speech assessment, transcription fidelity, and multimodal systems.

sections.heading: Open Source and Side Projects
sections.description: A mix of developer tools, ML experiments, and open-source utilities I have built over time. Some overlap with my research interests; many simply solve practical problems I care about.

taglines: I work on AI agents, software systems, and collaboration
I build tools, systems, and research prototypes
I study how people work together with AI
I also build open-source tools and ML projects
I am a 1st Year Graduate Student at NYCU
I like turning ideas into usable systems

promptChips.label: What are you studying now?
promptChips.prompt: What are your current research interests, and what problems are you trying to solve?

promptChips.label: Research interests
promptChips.prompt: What is your research focus across your current and past work?

promptChips.label: Your journey into tech
promptChips.prompt: How did you get into programming and technology? What shaped your path?

promptChips.label: How was this site built?
promptChips.prompt: What technologies and ideas went into building this personal site?

deepDivePrompts.label: Deep dive: Multi-human agent collaboration
deepDivePrompts.prompt: Deep dive into your work on multi-human steering for agent systems. How do you think teams should coordinate, align, and review shared agents without forcing one person to understand everything?

deepDivePrompts.label: Deep dive: Agent-driven software engineering
deepDivePrompts.prompt: Explain your research on agent-driven software engineering. What new development environments, interfaces, and visualizations do you think we need once agents can change code faster than humans can track?

deepDivePrompts.label: Deep dive: Faster software evolution
deepDivePrompts.prompt: Walk me through your thinking on faster software evolution in the Agent era. How can end-user needs move closer to real product changes without losing safety, controllability, or human understanding?

deepDivePrompts.label: Ask about the challenge system
deepDivePrompts.prompt: Can you walk me through your S&I Challenge 2025 system, what signals it combined, and what you learned from building it for the shared task?

deepDivePrompts.label: Ask about speaking assessment design
deepDivePrompts.prompt: Can you explain the motivation behind combining relevance and grammar information in automated speaking assessment, and what the experiments showed?

deepDivePrompts.label: Ask about hesitation tagging
deepDivePrompts.prompt: Why is acoustically precise hesitation tagging important for end-to-end verbatim transcription, and what changed when you modeled it explicitly?

deepDivePrompts.label: Ask about d1-manager
deepDivePrompts.prompt: What problems were you trying to solve with d1-manager, and how did you design the interface for inspecting and querying Cloudflare D1 databases?

deepDivePrompts.label: Ask about smart-whisper
deepDivePrompts.prompt: How does smart-whisper wrap whisper.cpp for Node.js, and what engineering decisions mattered for making it practical?

deepDivePrompts.label: Ask about LeetCode-Stats-Card
deepDivePrompts.prompt: How does LeetCode-Stats-Card turn practice data into reusable visuals, and what tradeoffs mattered in the design?
